channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Heatwave shuts theatres and scanners
item.description: Theatres have shut and equipment has broken down as the heatwave causes widespread disruption across the NHS’s ageing estate.

item.title: Ex-NHSE strategy director joins major PR firm
item.description: Former NHS England executive board member Chris Hopson has been appointed to a senior advisory role at a major communications consultancy.

item.title: Exclusive: Another major trust sacks staff over snooping
item.description: Members of staff from yet another NHS trust have been sacked for inappropriately viewing patient medical records, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Behave properly or leave, trust tells staff
item.description: A trust grappling with cultural challenges has told staff it is “drawing a line in the sand” on “disrespectful, dismissive, or undermining behaviour”.

item.title: Families sue over care failures
item.description: More than a dozen families have taken legal action against the NHS over care failings in paediatric audiology services, HSJ has discovered.

item.title: Mackey: We’ll change exec contracts to ensure accountability for failings
item.description: NHS executives could have their contracts rewritten to ensure they can be held to account for any actions taken while working for previous employers, NHS England’s CEO has said.

item.title: Behaviour concerns ‘swept under carpet’ at struggling trust
item.description: One of the NHS’s worst-performing trusts was beset by poor behaviour “at all levels”, according to an external review obtained by HSJ.

item.title: Trusts must recheck 10 years’ worth of mortuary records
item.description: Trusts must check records stretching back to 2016 to ensure any failings that have taken place in their mortuaries have been reported to the regulator.

item.title: Snooping on records ‘worrying trend, not just isolated incidents’
item.description: High-profile cases of staff snooping on patients’ health records, as most recently exposed by HSJ, have revealed “a worrying trend” across the NHS rather than just isolated incidences, a watchdog chief has warned.

item.title: Updated: Nottingham families slam ‘abhorrent’ NHS leadership as execs threatened with prison
item.description: Families affected by the Nottingham maternity scandal have hit out at the “abhorrent” leadership of “senior staff” as the government revealed that staff who refuse to engage with future investigations could be sent to prison for up to two years.

item.title: DHSC instructed to reduce use of external contractors
item.description: The Department of Health and Social Care has been told to develop plans to in-house services currently supplied by external contractors.

item.title: ‘Fundamental failure’ sparks NHSE intervention at top 10 trust
item.description: A “fundamental failure in quality governance” has led NHS England to take enforcement action against one of England’s largest trusts.

item.title: What a Burnham premiership will mean for the NHS
item.description: HSJ has spent the past few weeks talking to those in and around the “Burnham camp” in an effort to answer this question. Some consistent and significant themes have emerged.

item.title: Regulator launches statutory inquiry into private provider
item.description: The Charity Commission has launched an inquiry into one of the largest private mental health providers over safeguarding and financial concerns.

item.title: National quality strategy facing ‘ministerial pushback’
item.description: Serious concerns have been raised that the delayed NHS “quality strategy” does not “prioritise patient safety”, HSJ has discovered.

item.title: Judge advanced AI systems like doctors, says government review
item.description: Oversight of advanced AI systems capable of making autonomous decisions should “mirror” the assessment of healthcare professionals, a government commission has proposed.

item.title: Arrests made at maternity inquiry trust
item.description: Two men have been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office in connection with operating practices at Nottingham University Hospitals Trust’s mortuary service, police have revealed.

item.title: ‘Ham-fisted’ IT rollout ‘threatens service disruption’
item.description: NHS England is being warned that the planned rollout of a new “portal” for all NHS primary dental work could lead to widespread disruption.

item.title: Revealed: More than 1,400 record snooping cases reported to regulator
item.description: A watchdog has received more than 1,400 reports of unauthorised access of patient records over recent years, HSJ can reveal, with an influential MP urging government to take action.

item.title: ICB clusters told to merge
item.description: Clustered integrated care boards should merge by next April, even if it is likely they will cut across future regional government footprints, NHS England has said.
